finding: Self-consistency RANKING replicates across two independent model families (AUROC 0.65 DeepSeek, 0.64 Qwen) with near-perfect grading (kappa 0.97 / 0.99). The selective-accuracy LIFT is VALIDATED on DeepSeek (+0.158, CI [0.098,0.221]) but NOT on Qwen (+0.072, CI [-0.035,0.191] includes 0) — because Qwen self-abstains on 67% of SimpleQA vs DeepSeek's 6%, leaving only 196 attempted (underpowered at 20% coverage) and less headroom. The 3 Qwen seeds are STABLE (lifts 0.059/0.081/0.073), so non-significance is a POWER issue, not instability. Honest conclusion: a self-consistency conformal gate adds large value for an OVERCONFIDENT model that answers everything, and little for a CAUTIOUS model that already abstains heavily. Cross-model magnitude depends on base abstention behavior.
honestBound: DeepSeek validated; Qwen directional-only (underpowered). canClaimAGI false.
